meta.topic: Should in that-clauses; the present subjunctive

step_1_context.text: We can sometimes report advice, orders, requests, suggestions, etc. about things that need to be done or are desirable using a that-clause with should + bare infinitive:

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: We can sometimes report advice, orders, requests, suggestions, etc.
step_2_explanation.formula: In formal contexts, particularly in written English, we can often leave out should and use only the bare infinitive.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake: We can also use that-clauses with should or the subjunctive after reporting clauses with nouns related to the verbs in B (e.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 39.1.1 Lee felt that business in South America ___ (should) ___ (expand - passive).
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Passive form after 'should': be + past participle.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 39.1.2 It would be valuable for us to send a sales representative to South Africa. -> Alice recommended that a sales representative ___ to South Africa.
39.2.1 Look at this sentence: 'Lee felt that business in South America should be expanded.' Can it be written without 'should' (subjunctive)?
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: With 'feel' we don't usually use the subjunctive.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 39.3.1 It is ___ / she / marry Ben. (Select the most appropriate adjective for a strong recommendation/necessity).
options: inconceivable that she should marry Ben.
amused that she should marry Ben.
upset that she should marry Ben.
shocked that she should marry Ben.
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: inconceivable that she should marry Ben.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Inconceivable fits the structural prompt well.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 39.3.2 I am ___ / Kristina / behave so badly. (Select adj expressing emotional reaction).
options: shocked that Kristina should behave so badly.
imperative that Kristina should behave so badly.
urgent that Kristina should behave so badly.
vital that Kristina should behave so badly.
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: shocked that Kristina should behave so badly.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: 'Shocked' expresses an emotional reaction to a fact, followed by a should-clause.
